name: Pork Hocks (Schweinshaxe)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and dice the leek, celery, carrot, and onion.
2. Cook pork hocks, diced vegetables, salt, and peppercorns in enough water to cover for 2 to 3 hours, or until tender. Avoid overcooking. Remove from water; drain well, reserving vegetables and cooking liquid.
3. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
4. Melt fat or shortening in an enamel-lined, cast-iron pan.
5. Add drained pork hocks, cooked vegetables, and a small amount of cooking liquid. Bake for 30 minutes.
6. Moisten meat frequently with more cooking liquid.
7. Before the meat is fully cooked, sprinkle with beer or water in which a good amount of salt has been dissolved. Add cumin to increase flavor, if desired.
8. Serve with potato or white bread dumplings or sauerkraut salad. In Bavaria, the juices and cooking liquid are strained and served as an accompanying sauce.
Note: Recipe from The Best of German Cooking by Edda Meyer-Berkhout.
